item.ptxt: Brake fluid is extremely damaging to painted surfaces. If brake fluid contacts painted surface, immediately wipe it off and flush the surface with water thoroughly.
Rear axle bolts are pre-coated with friction stabilizer. Do not reuse rear axle bolt, otherwise bolts may loosen.

s1.ptxt: Hoist vehicle and remove rear wheels.
Remove rear caliper and rear brake disc for each of right and left rear wheels.
Remove rear drive shaft assembly for each of right and left rear wheels.
Remove rear wheel hub assembly and rear brake disc dust cover for each of right and left rear wheels.
Remove exhaust center pipe.
Remove propeller shaft assembly.
Remove wheel speed sensor clamp (4), rear wheel speed sensor harness (5) and parking brake cable clamp (6) from each of right and left rear axle.
Remove parking brake cable for each of right and left rear wheels.
Disconnect brake pipes (1) from brake hoses (2), and then remove E-ring (3) as shown.
Disconnect rear shock absorber lower end from rear axle and remove rear coil springs.
While supporting rear axle at both ends, remove rear axle bolts (1), and then remove rear axle from vehicle body by lowering floor jack gradually.
Place rear axle on floor jacks.
Then install rear axle with new right and left rear axle bolts (1) and tighten bolts temporarily by hand.
Install rear coil spring and connect rear shock absorber lower end to rear axle.
Connect brake pipes to brake hoses, and then install E-rings. Tighten rear brake pipe flare nuts to specified torque.
Install rear wheel speed sensor clamp and parking brake cable clamp to rear axle.
Install parking brake cable for each of right and left rear wheels.
Install propeller shaft assembly.
Install exhaust center pipe.
Install rear wheel hub assembly and rear brake disc dust cover for each of right and left rear wheels.
Install rear drive shaft assembly for each of right and left rear wheels.
Install rear caliper and rear brake disc for each of right and left rear wheels.
After installing brake parts, bleed air from brake system.
Adjust parking brake.
Install rear wheels.
Lower hoist and bounce vehicle up and down several times to stabilize suspension.
Tighten rear shock absorber lower nuts and rear axle bolts to specified torques.
Tighten rear axle bolts to specified torque.
Check that rear brake are free from dragging and proper braking is obtained.
Check all installed parts for brake fluid leakage.
